nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Grene
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Green
meanings: English: either a nickname for someone who was fond of dressing in this color (Old English grēne) or was young or immature, or who had played the part of the ‘Green Man’ in the May Day celebrations, or a topographic name for someone who lived near a village green (Middle English grene, a transferred use of the color term). This is one of the most common and widespread of English surnames. In North America it has assimilated cognates from other languages, notably German Grün (see Gruen ) and Dutch Groen ; compare 7 below. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
English: alternatively, from a Middle English personal name Grene.
Irish: adopted for Ó hUainín ‘descendant of Uainín’, a personal name from a pet form of uaine ‘green’, see Honan .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

nameMeanings.name: Bridgnorth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Bridgeford
meanings: English: habitational name from Bridgford in Staffordshire, or East or West Bridgford in Nottinghamshire, and possibly Bridgeford in Northumberland, which are all named with Old English brycg ‘bridge’ + ford ‘ford’.
Scottish: habitational name probably from a lost or unidentified place in Kincardineshire or Aberdeenshire, where the surname is most frequent.

nameMeanings.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

en.lifeSummary: John de Grene of Bridgnorth was born in 1323, in Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. He had at least 1 son. He died in 1360, in Bridgnorth, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 37.

fr.lifeSummary: John de Grene of Bridgnorth est né en 1323, à Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1360, à Bridgnorth, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 37 ans.

de.lifeSummary: John de Grene of Bridgnorth wurde 1323 in Shropshire, England, United Kingdom geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1360 im Alter von 37 Jahren in Bridgnorth, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: John de Grene of Bridgnorth è nato il nel 1323 nel Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1360, nel Bridgnorth, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 37 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: John de Grene of Bridgnorth(은)는 1323년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르에서 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1360년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 브리지노르스에서 37세의 나이로 사망했다.
